summary: Bangladesh's finances and governance dominated today's news as the government unveiled an ambitious FY27 budget amid criticism from economists and opposition parties over unrealistic revenue targets and risks of inflation without productivity gains. Bank officials and the central bank governor addressed ongoing financial sector challenges, while authorities continued special operations against crime, including a reported shooting of a BNP leader in Khulna. Meanwhile, Bangladesh highlighted its regional relationships with India's new high commissioner arriving in Dhaka and the BGB pressing India's border force to stop cross-border "push-ins" at a bilateral conference. International coverage focused on World Cup football action elsewhere, though local stories included employment scams affecting Bangladeshi workers rescued from Cambodia.
